---
title: "Phenotypic_plasticity_data"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-06"
---


```{r}
## ------------------------------------------------------------
## Setup
## ------------------------------------------------------------
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(tidyverse)
  library(biomaRt)
  library(glue)
})

## paths (edit if needed)
f_ens <- "data/ucsc_hg38_gene_known_genecodeV48"         # screenshot 1 (no extension)
f_ref <- "data/ucsc_hg38_gene_known_RefSeqALL.txt"       # screenshot 2

## ------------------------------------------------------------
## 1) Read UCSC tables and normalize ID versions in `name`
##    - Ensembl transcripts look like ENST00000...".1"
##    - RefSeq accessions look like NM_..., NR_..., XM_..., XR_...".2"
## ------------------------------------------------------------
ens_dt <- fread(f_ens, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, quote = "",
                na.strings = c("", "NA")) |>
  as_tibble()

## rename "#name" -> "name" if present
if ("#name" %in% names(ens_dt)) {
  ens_dt <- ens_dt |> rename(name = `#name`)
}

## strip transcript version (e.g., ENST...".1")
ens_dt <- ens_dt |>
  mutate(name_raw = name,
         name = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", name))

ref_dt <- fread(f_ref, sep = "\t", header = TRUE, quote = "",
                na.strings = c("", "NA")) |>
  as_tibble()

## rename "#bin" -> "bin" if present (optional), keep "name" as is
if ("#bin" %in% names(ref_dt)) {
  ref_dt <- ref_dt |> rename(bin = `#bin`)
}

## strip RefSeq version (e.g., NM_...".2")
ref_dt <- ref_dt |>
  mutate(name_raw = name,
         name = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", name))

cat(glue("Rows: Ensembl table = {nrow(ens_dt)}, RefSeq table = {nrow(ref_dt)}\n"))

## Quick sanity check
# safest: prefix dplyr verbs
ens_dt %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) %>%
  dplyr::select(name, chrom, strand) %>%
  print()

ref_dt %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) %>%
  dplyr::select(name, chrom, strand) %>%
  print()

```


```{r}
## ------------------------------------------------------------
## 2) Build an Ensembl→(HGNC, RefSeq) map with biomaRt
##    We query ENSEMBL GRCh38 and request:
##      - ensembl_transcript_id, ensembl_transcript_id_version
##      - ensembl_gene_id
##      - hgnc_symbol
##      - refseq_mrna, refseq_ncrna, and predicted variants
## ------------------------------------------------------------

## robust connection helper (falls back to GRCh38 archive if needed)
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr); library(purrr); library(tibble)
  library(biomaRt); library(glue)
})

## robust connection (as before)
.ens_connect <- function() {
  tryCatch(
    useEnsembl(biomart = "ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl"),
    error = function(e) useEnsembl(biomart = "ensembl",
                                   dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl",
                                   host = "https://grch38.ensembl.org")
  )
}
mart <- .ens_connect()

ens_ids <- unique(ens_dt$name)

## helper: batched getBM
bm_batched <- function(attrs, ids, mart, filter = "ensembl_transcript_id",
                       chunk_size = 50000) {
  split(ids, ceiling(seq_along(ids)/chunk_size)) |>
    map(\(vec) {
      getBM(attributes = attrs, filters = filter, values = vec, mart = mart) |>
        as_tibble()
    }) |>
    list_rbind()
}

## 1) core attributes (no external refs)
core_attrs <- c("ensembl_transcript_id",
                "ensembl_transcript_id_version",
                "ensembl_gene_id",
                "hgnc_symbol")

bm_core <- bm_batched(core_attrs, ens_ids, mart)

## 2) pull each RefSeq attribute separately, then bind long
ref_attrs <- c("refseq_mrna",            # NM_
               "refseq_mrna_predicted",  # XM_
               "refseq_ncrna",           # NR_
               "refseq_ncrna_predicted") # XR_

bm_ref_list <- map(ref_attrs, \(ra) {
  bm_batched(c("ensembl_transcript_id", ra), ens_ids, mart) |>
    rename(!!ra := all_of(ra))
})
```


```{r}
## merge core with concatenated ref columns
bm <- bm_core
for (tbl in bm_ref_list) {
  bm <- dplyr::left_join(bm, dplyr::distinct(tbl, ensembl_transcript_id, .keep_all = TRUE),
                         by = "ensembl_transcript_id")
}
bm <- bm %>% mutate(
  ensembl_transcript_id  = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", ensembl_transcript_id),
  refseq_mrna            = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", refseq_mrna),
  refseq_mrna_predicted  = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", refseq_mrna_predicted),
  refseq_ncrna           = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", refseq_ncrna),
  refseq_ncrna_predicted = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", refseq_ncrna_predicted)
)

# bm_long
bm_long <- bm %>%
  dplyr::select(
    ensembl_transcript_id, ensembl_gene_id, hgnc_symbol,
    refseq_mrna, refseq_mrna_predicted, refseq_ncrna, refseq_ncrna_predicted
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = tidyselect::starts_with("refseq_"),
    names_to = "refseq_type", values_to = "refseq_id"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(refseq_id), refseq_id != "") %>%
  dplyr::distinct()

## ------------------------------------------------------------
## 3) Merge mapping back to your Ensembl table
## ------------------------------------------------------------
# ENST -> HGNC (unique)
ens_map <- ens_dt %>%
  dplyr::select(name) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    bm %>% dplyr::select(ensembl_transcript_id, ensembl_gene_id, hgnc_symbol) %>%
      dplyr::distinct(),
    by = c("name" = "ensembl_transcript_id")
  )

## Attach RefSeqs (many-to-one); keep unique Ensembl–RefSeq pairs
# ENST -> RefSeq (many-to-one)
ens_to_refseq <- ens_dt %>%
  dplyr::select(name) %>%
  dplyr::distinct() %>%
  dplyr::left_join(
    bm_long %>% dplyr::select(ensembl_transcript_id, refseq_id) %>% dplyr::distinct(),
    by = c("name" = "ensembl_transcript_id")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(refseq_id)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct()
```


```{r}
## ------------------------------------------------------------
## 4) Compare against your UCSC RefSeq list
## ------------------------------------------------------------
# UCSC RefSeq list (version already stripped earlier)
refseq_ucsc <- ref_dt %>% dplyr::transmute(refseq_id = name) %>% dplyr::distinct()

# Overlap
## Overlap on accession (version-stripped)
ov_ids <- dplyr::inner_join(ens_to_refseq, refseq_ucsc, by = "refseq_id")

n_ens          <- n_distinct(ens_dt$name)
n_ens_mappedRS <- n_distinct(ens_to_refseq$name)       # ENST with ≥1 RefSeq
n_refseq_ucsc  <- nrow(refseq_ucsc)
n_refseq_from_ens <- n_distinct(ens_to_refseq$refseq_id)
n_overlap_refseq  <- nrow(distinct(ov_ids, refseq_id))

## Percentages of interest
pct_ens_with_refseq   <- 100 * n_ens_mappedRS / n_ens
pct_ucsc_refseq_seen  <- 100 * n_overlap_refseq / n_refseq_ucsc
pct_refseq_from_ens_in_ucsc <- 100 * n_overlap_refseq / n_refseq_from_ens

cat("\n===== Summary =====\n")
cat(glue::glue("Unique ENST in UCSC Ensembl file: {n_ens}\n"))
cat(glue::glue("ENST with ≥1 mapped RefSeq:       {n_ens_mappedRS} ({round(pct_ens_with_refseq,1)}%)\n\n"))

cat(glue::glue("Unique RefSeq in UCSC RefSeq file: {n_refseq_ucsc}\n"))
cat(glue::glue("Unique RefSeq from ENST mapping:   {n_refseq_from_ens}\n"))
cat(glue::glue("Overlap (RefSeq IDs):              {n_overlap_refseq}\n"))
cat(glue::glue("• % of UCSC RefSeq captured by ENST→RefSeq mapping: {round(pct_ucsc_refseq_seen,1)}%\n"))
cat(glue::glue("• % of mapped RefSeq that appear in UCSC RefSeqALL: {round(pct_refseq_from_ens_in_ucsc,1)}%\n\n"))
```


```{r}
## Optional: how many ENST still lack HGNC symbol or RefSeq?
missing_hgnc <- ens_map %>% summarize(n_missing = sum(is.na(hgnc_symbol)))
missing_refseq <- ens_dt %>%
  dplyr::select(name) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  anti_join(ens_to_refseq %>% distinct(name), by = "name") %>%
  nrow()

cat(glue::glue("ENST without HGNC symbol: {missing_hgnc$n_missing}\n"))
cat(glue::glue("ENST without any RefSeq:  {missing_refseq}\n"))

## ------------------------------------------------------------
## 5) Useful diagnostics to export
## ------------------------------------------------------------
## A) table of 1:many ENST→RefSeq (helps explain row-count differences)
one_to_many <- ens_to_refseq %>%
  count(name, name = "n_refseq") %>%
  count(n_refseq, name = "n_enst") %>%
  arrange(n_refseq)

print(one_to_many)

# ## B) write out mappings for audit
# write_tsv(ens_map,       "ENST_to_HGNC_map.tsv")
# write_tsv(ens_to_refseq, "ENST_to_RefSeq_map.tsv")
# write_tsv(ov_ids,        "Overlap_ENSTmapped_vs_UCSCRefSeq.tsv")

```
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(rtracklayer)
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
})

normalize_refseq <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  x <- toupper(trimws(x))
  sub("\\.\\d+$", "", x)   # drop version suffix
}

## (a) NCBI GTF IDs (transcripts only)
gtf_path <- "data/hg38.ncbiRefSeq.gtf.gz"  # or the unzipped .gtf
gr_all   <- rtracklayer::import(gtf_path)
ids_ncbi <- mcols(gr_all)$transcript_id
ids_ncbi <- ids_ncbi[!is.na(ids_ncbi)]
ids_ncbi <- ids_ncbi[stringr::str_detect(ids_ncbi, stringr::regex("^(NM|NR|XM|XR)_", ignore_case = TRUE))]
ids_ncbi <- normalize_refseq(ids_ncbi)
ids_ncbi <- unique(ids_ncbi)

## (b) Our mapped RefSeq set (ensure normalized)
ids_map <- ens_to_refseq %>%
  distinct(refseq_id) %>%
  pull() %>%
  normalize_refseq() %>%
  unique()

## (c) UCSC RefSeqALL IDs (normalize again just in case)
ids_ucsc <- ref_dt %>%
  distinct(name) %>%
  pull() %>%
  normalize_refseq() %>%
  unique()

## Overlaps
ov_map_ncbi  <- length(intersect(ids_map,  ids_ncbi))
ov_ncbi_ucsc <- length(intersect(ids_ncbi, ids_ucsc))

cat(sprintf("
--- ID overlap (version-stripped) ---
Mapped RefSeq (ENST→RefSeq): %d
NCBI GTF RefSeq IDs:         %d
UCSC RefSeqALL IDs:          %d

Overlap (Mapped ∩ NCBI):     %d  (%.1f%% of Mapped; %.1f%% of NCBI)
Overlap (NCBI ∩ UCSC):       %d  (%.1f%% of NCBI; %.1f%% of UCSC)
",
  length(ids_map), length(ids_ncbi), length(ids_ucsc),
  ov_map_ncbi,  100*ov_map_ncbi/length(ids_map),  100*ov_map_ncbi/length(ids_ncbi),
  ov_ncbi_ucsc, 100*ov_ncbi_ucsc/length(ids_ncbi), 100*ov_ncbi_ucsc/length(ids_ucsc)
))

```

```{r}
# Why your mapped set is much smaller
# 
# This is expected:
# 
# Cross-reference incompleteness at the transcript level. Ensembl↔RefSeq links don’t exist for many transcripts (especially XM/XR predicted models). Ensembl/Gencode v48 won’t carry a RefSeq cross-ref for a large fraction of RefSeq transcripts.
# 
# Input restriction. You only map RefSeq reachable from the ENSTs in your first file. Anything not in that ENST universe is unreachable.
# 
# Release mismatches. GENCODE v48 vs the Ensembl snapshot you queried vs the UCSC/NCBI files. Some transcripts were added/withdrawn/renamed.
# 
# Transcript vs gene mapping. We used transcript-level attributes (refseq_*). Many RefSeq transcripts are linked only at the gene level in Ensembl.
```
